summary: Backend-only testing of Quotex-clone Next.js API (via FastAPI reverse proxy). 39/39 pytest cases passed across auth, market data, trades (incl. auto-resolve), deposits/withdrawals (incl. admin approve/reject + escrow refund), admin (users, trades, settings, stats, force win/loss, balance adjust), announcements CRUD, support tickets full flow, leaderboard, and public settings.

minor.issue: MongoDB internal `_id` field is leaking in JSON responses (every list/get returns documents that still include `_id`). Should be excluded via projection ({_id: 0}) since the app already has its own UUID `id` field. Not breaking but exposes internal storage details and inflates payloads.

Endpoint named 'public' but actually requires Bearer auth (returns 401 to anonymous). Either rename to /api/settings/me or remove auth gate; current behaviour is misleading for clients that hit it pre-login (e.g., to fetch min deposit before signup).
Returns 400 with no password-strength validation. Accepts any non-empty password. Consider min length / complexity check.
Accepts empty/null outcome to clear force flag, which is fine, but does not return 404 if tradeId does not exist (silently returns null trade). Minor robustness gap.

action_items: Strip MongoDB `_id` from all admin list endpoints (use Mongo projection {_id: 0}) to avoid leaking ObjectId in API responses.
Decide whether /api/settings/public should be truly public; currently it 401s for unauthenticated requests despite its name.
Add minimum password length validation in /api/auth/signup.
Return 404 from /api/admin/trades/:id/force when trade is unknown.
critical_code_review_comments: All 19 endpoints listed in the test plan are functional. Auth (JWT + bcrypt + seed users), trade auto-resolution (5s trade closed within ~7s by background resolver), and deposit/withdrawal escrow/refund flows behave correctly.

route.js is 735 lines — single catch-all handler for ALL endpoints. Strongly recommend splitting per-feature (auth.js, trades.js, admin.js, support.js) for maintainability and to enable per-route testing without booting the whole engine.

Bootstrap (ensureSeedUsers, startEngine, startResolver, startLiveFeed) runs on first request via global flag. In Next.js dev with hot reload this is OK; in production multi-process this should move to instrumentation.ts or a dedicated worker to avoid duplicate background loops.
Withdrawal logic correctly escrows and refunds on reject — verified via test.

Admin force-win/loss only sets a flag (forceOutcome); actual application happens in tradeResolver. Could not validate end-to-end forcing without also waiting for resolution; flag persistence verified.
MongoDB `_id` exposure is the most repeated minor issue; trivial fix with projection.
test_credentials: admin@trading.com / password (admin), masteruser@trading.com / password (user); ad-hoc signup uses test_<uuid>@example.com / Password123!
seed_data_creation: Each pytest session creates 1 fresh user via /api/auth/signup, places small demo trades, creates a deposit (approved -> credits 100 to live), creates+rejects a withdrawal (refunded), creates+deletes an announcement and creates+closes a support ticket. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_quotex_backend.py (pytest, 39 cases, ~18s runtime). Uses BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (or reads /app/frontend/.env fallback). Session-scoped fixtures: admin_token, master_token, fresh_user (with 10000 demo balance + ~100 live after the deposit-approval test runs). Run via: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Tests are idempotent and clean up announcements; deposit/withdrawal/trade rows accumulate in DB but are user-scoped (test users are unique per run). Frontend (Next.js UI) was NOT tested — main agent requested backend-only.
rca of the issue: No bugs encountered. The single test failure was an incorrect OHLC field-name expectation in my own test (API returns open/high/low/close, not o/h/l/c) — fixed and re-run successfully.
